_purpose: Regression baseline for Phase 2.6 C2 (drop handle.lock + enable iws parallelism).
_scenario: 5 concurrent shell `sleep 0.2` calls fired against one already-open isolated workspace (single agent).

_pre_phase_2_6_observed_s.note: Pre-C2 wall time approx 5 x per_call_max plus per-call lock-acquire + freeze-pair overhead.

_post_phase_2_6_expected_s.note: Post-C2 wall time bounded by 1.5 x per_call_max because tool calls overlap inside the run_in_executor pool. The same regression assertion lives in tests/mock/sandbox/isolated_workspace/concurrency/test_same_agent_tool_calls_can_overlap.py (wall < 0.9 s for 2 x sleep 0.5).

_regression_signal: If the parallel wall-time floor regresses to >= 0.9 s for the 5 x 200 ms scenario (or >= 0.9 s for the 2 x 500 ms harness scenario), per-call serialization has been re-introduced and the contributor should hunt for a new handle-keyed lock, asyncio.Lock around run_in_executor, or a freeze/unfreeze pair around run_in_handle.
